textPlain: In 2021 I found an invisible memory leak in a tool (VS Code) that I have never used. This is the story of how.

> Sometimes I think it would be nice to have limits on resources in order to more automatically find mistakes like thisI was actually fairly disappointed when Visual Studio (not code) went to 64-bit.  Because I knew its memory usage was now going to be unconstrained.  Still way better than the unapologetic gluttony of Rider but experiences showed it to be a bit leaky over time (tip: Ctrl-Alt-Shift-F12 twice does a full garbage collection https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/visualstudio/ide/visual-st...)Also remember that all your references (pointers) are going to double in size so right off the bat it will use more, potentially a lot more depending on how reference-heavy your data is
Re the last wish, isn't that exactly what ulimit is for?
